preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for ForgeLink agent-human governance primitives.

acceptance_criteria.text: Add local-first Human Cards so agents can address human authority by alias/role rather than only by phone number or contact string.
Add authority scopes so approval requests declare required authority and ForgeLink can reject or escalate requests addressed to humans without that authority.
Add Agent Identity Registry for agents, systems, MCP clients, and local workflow sources, including source, owner, allowed tools/channels, default risk limit, escalation contact, and trust state.
Add agent trust states and probation so new agents are conservative by default, muted/blocked agents cannot interrupt, and trust changes are explicit/audited.
Add agent background-check checklist for promoting agents to trusted status based on source, owner, scopes, behavior, tool permissions, and validation history.
Add structured approval request schema with intent, requested action, reason for interrupt, risk, authority, affected resources, expiration, timeout behavior, deny behavior, and decision options.
Add Evidence Packs for approval requests, including summary, affected resources, diff summary, proposed operation, checks, rollback plan, links, limitations, and channel-specific redaction.

Add approval templates/playbooks for file writes, data deletion, git commits, GitHub releases, mirror syncs, external messages, credential changes, network access, purchases, and provider setting changes.

Add approval dry-run/simulation so agents can learn whether approval is required, risk level, missing evidence, preferred channel, batching/defer recommendation, and validation errors before interrupting.
Add risk-tiered interruption policy covering log-only, passive notification, normal approval, urgent interrupt, fail-closed critical approval, and multi-party/cannot-proceed states.
Add explicit timeout and escalation behavior for every approval class, including deny, defer, retry, channel escalation, operator escalation, and fail-closed handling.
Add agent etiquette protocol requiring urgency, reason for interrupt, expected response time, no-response behavior, batching, and can-wait-until fields.
Add Decision Records that persist what the human saw and decided, including request/evidence hashes, operator identity, device identity, decision, timestamp, options, comments, and authority grant.
Add Decision Memory that detects repeated approval patterns and suggests future policy only with explicit operator confirmation.
Add outcome callbacks so agents report action started/succeeded/failed/expired/cancelled/modified-scope outcomes after approval.
Add tamper-evident local audit chain for approval requests, evidence packs, decisions, and outcomes using practical local hash-linked records.
Add approval replay showing request received, risk classified, evidence shown, decision made, action started, outcome reported, and final state.
Add redacted governance export for approval/audit history, excluding credentials and private message bodies by default.
Add communication firewall rules controlling how agents may communicate with humans and external channels before dispatch.
Add draft-don't-send mode for external channels so agents can draft messages while human review/send remains explicit and audited.
Add external-contact consent ledger covering allowed topics, channels, hours, review requirements, consent source, and last review.
Add redaction profiles for desktop, mobile lock screen, SMS fallback, email, Discord/status-only, and other channel surfaces.

Reconcile the automatic public webhook tunnel (item 014) with the private-first posture: document the inbound public attack surface, ensure only provider-signature-validated requests reach handlers, rate-limit and lock down the publicly exposed webhook routes, scope exactly what the tunnel exposes, and record a decision on the tunnel boundary.

Treat agent-supplied content (evidence packs, approval text, agent messages) as untrusted input: label it as agent-provided/unverified in the UI, never auto-execute or auto-trust it, sanitize/escape it, and design the approval surface to resist social engineering such as spoofed system text, fabricated urgency, or look-alike operator prompts.

Define key management for decision signing and the tamper-evident audit chain: key and device-key generation, OS-backed secure storage, rotation, lost-device revocation, and recovery, and state the actual integrity guarantee the chain provides.

Specify the agent-facing governance contract: how an agent submits an approval request, awaits a decision, and receives the outcome, mapped onto the MCP bridge and ForgeWire Fabric dispatch/await_result, including timeouts, deferral, and outcome reporting, so agents have a concrete contract to consume.

Add one end-to-end governance-loop integration test exercising request -> risk classification -> evidence -> decision -> outcome -> audit -> replay, proving the full lifecycle holds together beyond per-primitive unit tests.

Pipe ForgeWire Fabric human-in-the-loop approvals through ForgeLink: when ForgeLink is installed and reachable, Fabric's HITL approval pane automatically routes approval requests to ForgeLink as the governed decision surface (evidence packs, audit, redaction, mobile companion), with an explicit operator opt-out and graceful fallback to Fabric's built-in pane when ForgeLink is absent.
